Tests that the reciprocal of the square root of negative zero is negative infinity.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of the JavaScript `Math.sqrt` function when applied to negative zero. It verifies that dividing 1 by the square root of negative zero results in negative infinity, as expected by the IEEE 754 standard for floating-point arithmetic. The assertion uses the `assert` function to ensure that the expression evaluates to `true`, which helps in validating the correctness of the mathematical operation. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific edge case in mathematical computations.
